signals.label: Apigee Gateway on AWS Backend
signals.detail: api.investec.com CNAMEs through investec-prod-production.apigee.net and reu1rtd100-0-routers.dn.apigee.net before resolving to AWS IP 34.250.63.54, meaning cardholder data flows through both GCP-managed Apigee and AWS infrastructure.
signals.emailAngle: We noticed api.investec.com routes through Apigee (investec-prod-production.apigee.net) before hitting your AWS backend — in PCI DSS assessments, this dual-hop architecture often creates ambiguity around where your CDE boundary sits and which provider falls in scope.

signals.label: Cloudflare, AWS, and Apigee in Scope
signals.detail: Production traffic spans Cloudflare (CDN/WAF), Google Apigee (API gateway), and AWS (origin compute at 34.250.63.54), meaning PCI DSS scope must be demonstrated across three distinct provider environments for a single API transaction.
signals.emailAngle: Investec's production API path touches Cloudflare, Apigee, and AWS in a single request chain — we specialise in helping financial institutions map and evidence PCI DSS scope across exactly this kind of multi-provider architecture.

signals.label: Exposed Staging and Dev Subdomains
signals.detail: Both staging.investec.com and dev.investec.com are publicly resolvable and Cloudflare-fronted (104.16.203.57 / 104.16.204.57), sharing the same IP range as production — a common PCI DSS audit finding when non-prod environments are not demonstrably isolated from the CDE.
signals.emailAngle: Your staging.investec.com and dev.investec.com subdomains are publicly reachable and resolve to the same Cloudflare IP range as production — QSAs routinely flag this as evidence that non-prod environment segregation needs formal documentation under PCI DSS Requirement 12.5.2.

signals.label: Mimecast Email Security (ZA Region)
signals.detail: MX records point exclusively to za-smtp-inbound-1.mimecast.co.za and za-smtp-inbound-2.mimecast.co.za, confirming South Africa-region Mimecast deployment, which indicates a ZA-domiciled security stack alongside a globally distributed cloud infrastructure.
signals.emailAngle: Investec's email is routed through South African Mimecast endpoints, suggesting your security operations are ZA-anchored — we can help align your cloud PCI DSS compliance posture to both SARB and PCI SSC requirements given this cross-jurisdictional infrastructure.

signals.label: secure.investec.com Hardened Separately
signals.detail: secure.investec.com resolves to a distinct Cloudflare IP pair (104.18.191.234 / 104.18.190.234) separate from other subdomains, suggesting deliberate network segmentation for what is likely a sensitive banking or payment-facing surface.
signals.emailAngle: secure.investec.com sits on a distinct Cloudflare IP range from your other properties — if this subdomain handles any payment or authentication flows, we can help you formally document it as a scoped CDE asset with the evidence trail a QSA will require.

headline: Investec's api.investec.com resolves through Apigee (investec-prod-production.apigee.net) on AWS, creating a multi-layer API compliance surface that spans Cloudflare, GCP's Apigee, and AWS — a classic PCI DSS scope creep scenario for a regulated financial institution.
hostingSummary: Investec hosts its API backend on AWS (34.250.63.54 via api.investec.com) with front-end and non-prod environments fronted by Cloudflare, and API traffic routed through Google's Apigee gateway.
